title: Mastering `font-display` for Superior Web Font Performance & UX
tweet: Boost your site's performance & user experience! Learn how to effectively use `font-display` for web fonts to prevent FOIT/FOUT. Your typography will thank you. #CSSTypography #WebFonts #UIDesign #UX
tip: Optimize your web font loading strategy by thoughtfully implementing the `font-display` CSS property within your `@font-face` declarations. Instead of relying on the default 'auto' (which often behaves like 'block' leading to a Flash of Invisible Text - FOIT), consider 'swap' for rapid content display, or 'fallback' for a short block period before swapping to prevent abrupt layout shifts. For less critical fonts, 'optional' can defer loading until the connection is stable, ensuring a smooth experience. This significantly improves perceived performance and prevents jarring layout shifts, enhancing the overall user experience and core web vitals.
